Interface ITextInputEditor

Describes an interface for a text input editor.

The ITextInputEditor interface is implemented by the UI library and is not intended to be implemented by consumer code.

Inherited Members
IInputEditor.EditValueChanged
IInputEditor.EditValueChanging
IInputEditor.EditValue
IInputEditor.ErrorText
IInputEditor.ErrorIconAlignment
IInputEditor.RichToolTip
IInputEditor.ShowToolTips
IInputEditor.ToolTipController
ISynchronizeInvoke.BeginInvoke(Delegate, object[])
ISynchronizeInvoke.EndInvoke(IAsyncResult)
ISynchronizeInvoke.Invoke(Delegate, object[])
ISynchronizeInvoke.InvokeRequired
IDisposable.IsDisposed
IComponent.Site
IComponent.Disposed
IDisposable.Dispose()
IWin32Window.Handle
Namespace: PhiRobotics.UI.Editors
Assembly: PhiRobotics.UI.dll
Syntax
public interface ITextInputEditor : IInputEditor, ISynchronizeInvoke, IDisposable, IComponent, IDisposable, IWin32Window
Remarks

The ITextInputEditor interface extends IInputEditor and provides functionality for editing textual values with optional masking and formatting.

This editor supports multiple input modes through mask configuration, allowing you to restrict input, enforce formats, and control how values are displayed and stored.

Use the Configure(Type, EditMaskType) method to define the value type and mask behavior. Depending on the configuration, the editor exposes different MaskProperties implementations.

Instances of ITextInputEditor are created using the IInputEditorFactory.

Examples

The following example demonstrates how to configure a text editor:

ITextInputEditor editor = factory.CreateTextEdit();

editor.NullValuePrompt = "Enter value...";
editor.NullValuePromptForeColor = Color.LightSkyBlue;
editor.BeepOnError = true;
editor.UseMaskAsDisplayFormat = false;

Properties

BeepOnError

Gets or sets whether the editor plays a system error sound when invalid input is entered.

Declaration
bool BeepOnError { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
bool

true to play a system sound on invalid input; otherwise, false.

MaskProperties

Gets the mask settings that control user input.

Declaration
ITextEditorMaskProperties MaskProperties { get; }
Property Value
Type Description
ITextEditorMaskProperties

An instance of ITextEditorMaskProperties.

NullValuePrompt

Gets or sets the placeholder text displayed when the editor’s value is null or empty.

Declaration
string NullValuePrompt { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
string

A string that specifies the placeholder text.

NullValuePromptForeColor

Gets or sets the color of the placeholder text.

Declaration
Color NullValuePromptForeColor { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
Color

A Color value that specifies the placeholder text color.

UseMaskAsDisplayFormat

Gets or sets whether the input mask is also used to format the display value when the editor is not focused.

Declaration
bool UseMaskAsDisplayFormat { get; set; }
Property Value
Type Description
bool

true to use the mask for both display and edit modes; otherwise, false.

Methods

Configure(Type, EditMaskType)

Configures the editor to accept values of the specified valueType and applies the specified mask.

Declaration
void Configure(Type valueType = null, EditMaskType mask = EditMaskType.None)
Parameters
Type Name Description
Type valueType

A Type that specifies the type of values accepted by the editor. If null, the editor accepts string values.
EditMaskType mask

An EditMaskType value that specifies the mask type to apply.
Remarks

When valueType is null, the editor is configured for string input and the MaskProperties instance is initialized based on the specified mask.

When valueType is not null, the editor initializes mask settings appropriate for the specified type (for example, numeric or date/time input).

Events

CustomDisplayText

Raised when custom display text is required for the editor.

Declaration
event EventHandler<InputCustomDisplayTextEventArgs> CustomDisplayText
Event Type
Type Description
EventHandler<InputCustomDisplayTextEventArgs>
Remarks

The event data is provided by InputCustomDisplayTextEventArgs.

This event is raised each time the editor loses focus, allowing you to customize how the value is displayed.

InvalidValue

Raised when an invalid value is entered.

Declaration
event EventHandler<InvalidInputExceptionEventArgs> InvalidValue
Event Type
Type Description
EventHandler<InvalidInputExceptionEventArgs>
Remarks

The event data is provided by InvalidInputExceptionEventArgs.

ParseEditValue

Raised when the editor converts an input value to its internal representation.

Declaration
event EventHandler<InputConvertEditValueEventArgs> ParseEditValue
Event Type
Type Description
EventHandler<InputConvertEditValueEventArgs>
Remarks

The event data is provided by InputConvertEditValueEventArgs.

This event is raised when a user changes the value or when a new value is assigned programmatically, allowing custom conversion logic.

QueryProcessKey

Raised when a key press is evaluated to determine whether it should be processed by the editor or by its container.

Declaration
event EventHandler<QueryInputProcessKeyEventArgs> QueryProcessKey
Event Type
Type Description
EventHandler<QueryInputProcessKeyEventArgs>
Remarks

The event data is provided by QueryInputProcessKeyEventArgs.

This event allows you to override default keyboard handling behavior.
